question: Can I stake my XLM for passive income, and are there plans for native staking?

golden.answer: Stellar does not use proof-of-stake, so holding XLM does not natively stake it or earn protocol staking rewards. If a third party advertises 'XLM staking,' it is offering its own product, not activating a native network feature; investigate its custody, lockup, withdrawal, counterparty, and scam risks independently. Never send a secret key or funds to a promoter just to 'activate' staking. As of 2026-07-11, current Protocol 27 materials and a live sweep of the official accepted and proposed CAP repository show no native-staking or validator-reward proposal. That is a dated record claim, not a promise that future governance can never change.
keyFacts: States that Stellar has no native proof-of-stake reward for holding XLM.
Distinguishes a third-party yield product from native staking.
avoid: Do NOT say SDF runs an official staking program.
Do NOT call every third-party yield product native staking.
Do NOT say native staking will never exist or present an undated absence-of-plans claim.
golden.notes: Negative/status claim requires blind re-derivation before landing.

sources.note: Repository sweep pinned to commit fbf05c9d3220b711e181577e7dca19844c765c3c.
Independent consumer-protection source for third-party yield/scam risk; it does not establish Stellar protocol status.

corroboration.claim: Stellar consensus is federated Byzantine agreement rather than proof of stake, so holding XLM does not earn native validator rewards.
No native-staking or validator-reward CAP was present in the official accepted/proposed repository sweep as of 2026-07-11.

evidence.note: Live repository sweep pinned to commit fbf05c9d3220b711e181577e7dca19844c765c3c; source-relative absence claim, not a claim about all future governance.

verified.by: P4 H3 primary+blind (Solo 3415/3419) with Fable voice review
